Title: Validate patient dashboard render p95 under nominal load
Preconditions: Owned perf environment with production-like data set (>=250k patients); load tool (JMeter/k6) configured; APM (e.g. New Relic/Grafana) capturing percentiles
Test_Steps: 1. Warm caches with a 5-min ramp | 2. Drive 50 concurrent virtual users opening the patient dashboard | 3. Sustain steady state for 15 min | 4. Capture server-side and end-to-end latency percentiles
Expected_Result: p50<=800ms AND p95<=2000ms AND p99<=3500ms for dashboard render; no errors; results recorded in APM

Title: Validate login page p99 latency budget is not exceeded under load
Preconditions: Owned perf environment; auth service reachable; percentile capture enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Ramp to 100 concurrent login attempts over 2 min | 2. Sustain for 10 min | 3. Record p50/p95/p99 for the authenticate call | 4. Compare against SLO budget
Expected_Result: p99 for authenticate <=1500ms; p95<=900ms; no auth timeouts; lockout logic unaffected

Title: Measure REST patient search API latency percentiles at volume
Preconditions: Owned env with >=250k patients indexed; REST API token; percentile capture
Test_Steps: 1. Issue patient search GET with common surname query | 2. Drive 75 req/s for 10 min | 3. Capture latency distribution | 4. Verify result count stability
Expected_Result: p95<=1200ms AND p99<=2500ms; result set deterministic; no 5xx; no connection pool exhaustion

Title: Confirm latency SLO breach is detected and alerted, not silently passed
Preconditions: Owned env; alerting wired to percentile thresholds; ability to inject latency
Test_Steps: 1. Inject 1500ms artificial DB latency via toxiproxy | 2. Run patient search load | 3. Observe p95 cross the SLO threshold | 4. Verify alert fires
Expected_Result: p95 exceeds 1200ms; monitoring raises a breach alert within 60s; run is flagged FAIL; no false-green

Title: Determine sustained throughput ceiling for encounter save API
Preconditions: Owned env; clinician token; active visits pre-created; APM
Test_Steps: 1. Ramp encounter POSTs until error rate or latency knee | 2. Hold each step 5 min | 3. Record max stable RPS where p95<=SLO and errors<0.1% | 4. Capture saturated resource
Expected_Result: Max stable throughput documented (e.g. >=40 enc/s); knee point identified; saturated resource named (CPU/DB)

Title: Verify throughput does not collapse at the saturation knee (no cliff)
Preconditions: Owned env; throughput ceiling from TC-0005 known
Test_Steps: 1. Drive load to 95%, 100%, 105% of known ceiling | 2. Hold each 5 min | 3. Observe throughput vs offered load curve | 4. Check for negative scaling
Expected_Result: Throughput plateaus rather than collapsing; at 105% errors degrade gracefully, no >50% throughput drop (thrashing)

Title: Validate 500 concurrent clinical users mixed workload
Preconditions: Owned env sized for prod; realistic workload model; APM; representative data
Test_Steps: 1. Build mixed scenario (40% search, 25% dashboard, 20% encounter, 10% orders, 5% reports) | 2. Ramp to 500 concurrent users over 10 min | 3. Sustain 30 min | 4. Capture per-transaction percentiles and error rate
Expected_Result: All transaction p95 within SLO; aggregate error rate<0.5%; no session leakage; system stable through soak

Title: Pairwise combinations of user role x workload x data volume
Preconditions: Owned env; roles seeded (clinician/nurse/registrar/pharmacist); volume tiers prepared
Test_Steps: 1. Construct pairwise matrix across role, workload type, and data tier | 2. Execute each pair at 100 VUs | 3. Capture latency per cell | 4. Flag any cell breaching SLO
Test_Data: Roles=4; Workloads=5; Volume=3 tiers; pairwise reduced set
Expected_Result: No pairwise combination breaches its transaction SLO; results table populated; outliers root-caused

Title: Confirm graceful rejection beyond max concurrent session cap
Preconditions: Owned env; max session/connection limits configured
Test_Steps: 1. Ramp connections past configured max | 2. Observe excess connection handling | 3. Verify rejected users get a clear error, not a hang | 4. Confirm existing sessions unaffected
Expected_Result: Excess requests rejected with HTTP 503/429 promptly; admitted sessions keep SLO; no deadlock; recovery on backoff

Title: Patient search latency by surname with high-cardinality result set
Preconditions: Owned env with >=250k patients; many duplicate surnames seeded
Test_Steps: 1. Search a very common surname returning >1000 candidates | 2. Apply default pagination | 3. Measure first-page latency under 30 VUs | 4. Verify index usage
Expected_Result: First page p95<=1500ms; only first page materialized; DB uses name index; no full table scan

Title: Search with minimum-length and wildcard-heavy term at volume
Preconditions: Owned env at volume; search min-length policy known
Test_Steps: 1. Issue single-character and two-character queries | 2. Issue leading-wildcard query | 3. Measure latency and result truncation | 4. Confirm guardrails
Expected_Result: Sub-min queries rejected or capped per policy; leading wildcard either blocked or bounded; p95 stays within SLO; no runaway scan

Title: Encounter list pagination latency for a heavy chart
Preconditions: Owned env; a patient seeded with >5000 encounters
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient chart | 2. Page through encounter list (page size 25) | 3. Measure per-page latency for pages 1, 50, 200 | 4. Verify deep-page stability
Expected_Result: Per-page p95<=1500ms including deep pages; no O(n) deep-page degradation; keyset/index pagination evident

Title: Validate keyset vs offset pagination behavior at deep offsets
Preconditions: Owned env; DB query logging on; large list endpoint available
Test_Steps: 1. Capture SQL for page 1 and page 500 | 2. Compare query plan and execution time | 3. Check for large OFFSET scans | 4. Record rows examined
Expected_Result: Deep-page query does not linearly increase rows examined; no large OFFSET full scan; execution time bounded

Title: Reject malformed/oversized page-size parameters
Preconditions: Owned env; REST API reachable
Test_Steps: 1. Request limit=0, limit=-1, limit=100000, limit=abc | 2. Observe response | 3. Verify server caps page size | 4. Confirm no memory spike
Expected_Result: Invalid values rejected (400) or clamped to max cap; no unbounded result materialization; heap stable

Title: Measure aggregate clinical report generation time at data volume
Preconditions: Owned env at volume; reporting module configured; representative report defined
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a cohort/aggregate report over 12 months | 2. Run with no concurrent load, then under 50 VU background | 3. Measure wall-clock generation time | 4. Capture DB time share
Expected_Result: Report completes within agreed budget (e.g. <=120s); generation time documented; under background load <=1.5x baseline

Title: Report generation at maximum permitted date range / cohort size
Preconditions: Owned env; max report window policy known; large cohort seeded
Test_Steps: 1. Run report at max allowed date range and max cohort | 2. Measure time and memory | 3. Verify no timeout/OOM | 4. Check streaming vs full-buffer output
Expected_Result: Report completes or is paginated/streamed; no OOM; if over policy limit, user gets clear bounded-range message

Title: Concurrent heavy reports must not starve interactive clinical traffic
Preconditions: Owned env; report queue/throttle configured; interactive SLO known
Test_Steps: 1. Launch 5 concurrent heavy reports | 2. Simultaneously run interactive clinical workload | 3. Measure interactive p95 during report run | 4. Verify isolation/throttle
Expected_Result: Interactive clinical p95 stays within SLO; reports queued/throttled; no clinical workflow starvation

Title: 72-hour soak for memory leak and resource creep
Preconditions: Owned env; long-run monitoring; heap/GC and connection metrics captured
Test_Steps: 1. Apply steady moderate load (40% of ceiling) | 2. Run continuously 72h | 3. Trend heap, GC pause, DB connections, file handles | 4. Compare end-vs-start baselines
Expected_Result: No sustained upward memory/handle creep; GC pauses stable; latency p95 flat over 72h; no restarts required

Title: State-transition: system stays in STEADY across soak, never enters DEGRADED
Preconditions: Owned env; degradation states defined (Steady/Degraded/Saturated/Recovering)
Test_Steps: 1. Define state model and thresholds | 2. Run soak | 3. Log state transitions | 4. Verify only Steady observed barring planned events
Expected_Result: System remains in Steady; any Degraded transition is alerted and auto-Recovers; no Saturated state during soak

Title: Sudden 10x traffic spike recovery behavior
Preconditions: Owned env; baseline throughput known; autoscale/queue config in place
Test_Steps: 1. Run baseline 5 min | 2. Inject instantaneous 10x spike for 3 min | 3. Return to baseline | 4. Measure latency spike, error burst, and time-to-recover
Expected_Result: System absorbs or sheds load gracefully; errors bounded; latency recovers to baseline within agreed RTO (e.g. <=2min) after spike ends

Title: Decision table for spike response by autoscale x queue x cache state
Preconditions: Owned env; autoscale, request queue, and cache toggles configurable
Test_Steps: 1. Enumerate combinations of autoscale on/off, queue on/off, warm/cold cache | 2. Apply identical spike to each | 3. Record outcome (absorb/shed/fail) | 4. Build decision table
Expected_Result: Each combination's outcome matches expected; cold-cache+no-queue is worst case and is documented; no combination causes data loss

Title: Spike must not cause duplicate or lost encounter writes
Preconditions: Owned env; idempotency/retry behavior known; write workload prepared
Test_Steps: 1. During spike, drive encounter POSTs with client retries | 2. Inject transient 503s | 3. After recovery, reconcile written encounters vs intended | 4. Check duplicates
Expected_Result: No duplicate encounters from retries (idempotency holds); no lost writes; counts reconcile exactly post-recovery

Title: Stress to failure to find breaking point and failure mode
Preconditions: Owned env isolated from prod; ability to push to failure; full telemetry
Test_Steps: 1. Ramp load continuously beyond ceiling until first hard failure | 2. Record breaking-point load | 3. Identify failure mode (DB pool/CPU/OOM/thread starvation) | 4. Confirm no data corruption
Expected_Result: Breaking point quantified; failure mode is graceful (rejects) not catastrophic (corruption/crash-loop); recovery procedure validated

Title: State-transition: Saturated -> Recovering -> Steady after stress removed
Preconditions: Owned env; stress test from TC-0023 reproducible
Test_Steps: 1. Drive system into Saturated state | 2. Remove load | 3. Observe recovery without manual restart | 4. Time return to Steady
Expected_Result: System self-recovers to Steady within RTO; queues drain; no stuck Saturated state; latency normalizes; no leaked resources

Title: Enforce per-request DB query count budget (no N+1) on dashboard
Preconditions: Owned env; SQL/ORM query logging or APM DB tracing enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Load patient dashboard | 2. Capture all SQL issued for the request | 3. Count queries and check for N+1 loops | 4. Compare against budget
Expected_Result: Query count within budget; no N+1 pattern (e.g. per-encounter lookups in a loop); slow queries indexed

Title: Heavy chart must not exceed query budget proportionally to history size
Preconditions: Owned env; light vs heavy patients seeded (10 vs 5000 encounters)
Test_Steps: 1. Load dashboard for light patient, capture query count | 2. Load for heavy patient, capture query count | 3. Compare scaling | 4. Flag linear growth
Expected_Result: Query count near-constant or bounded regardless of history size; no per-row query growth; pagination limits fetched rows

Title: Identify and budget slow queries under load
Preconditions: Owned env; DB slow-query log enabled with threshold
Test_Steps: 1. Run mixed load 15 min | 2. Collect slow-query log entries above threshold | 3. Rank by total time | 4. Verify each has supporting index
Expected_Result: Top slow queries identified; each backed by an index or justified; no unindexed full scans on hot paths under load

Title: Connection pool sizing under peak concurrency
Preconditions: Owned env; pool metrics exposed; peak concurrency model
Test_Steps: 1. Drive peak concurrent load | 2. Monitor active/idle/waiting connections | 3. Check for pool exhaustion and wait time | 4. Verify timeout handling
Expected_Result: Pool not exhausted at peak; wait time near zero; no connection-acquire timeouts; sizing headroom documented

Title: Validate cache hit ratio for reference/concept data under load
Preconditions: Owned env; cache metrics exposed; concept dictionary populated
Test_Steps: 1. Warm cache | 2. Run read-heavy clinical load | 3. Measure cache hit ratio for concept/metadata lookups | 4. Compare cold vs warm latency
Expected_Result: Hit ratio>=90% for reference data after warm-up; warm latency materially lower than cold; DB read load reduced

Title: Cache invalidation must not serve stale clinical data
Preconditions: Owned env; cached entity that can be edited (e.g. order set/concept)
Test_Steps: 1. Read a cached entity (note value) | 2. Update the entity via admin | 3. Re-read immediately | 4. Verify fresh value, not stale cache
Expected_Result: Post-update reads return fresh value within invalidation window; no stale clinical data served; TTL/invalidation correct

Title: Pairwise cache config (TTL x size x eviction) latency impact
Preconditions: Owned env; cache TTL, max size, eviction policy configurable
Test_Steps: 1. Build pairwise set of TTL/size/eviction | 2. Run identical load per config | 3. Record hit ratio and p95 | 4. Identify best config
Expected_Result: Each config's hit ratio and latency recorded; no config serves stale data; recommended config documented

Title: Establish capacity headroom at expected peak (target 40% headroom)
Preconditions: Owned env sized like prod; expected peak load model agreed
Test_Steps: 1. Run at projected peak load | 2. Measure CPU/mem/DB/IO utilization | 3. Compute headroom to saturation | 4. Document scaling trigger thresholds
Expected_Result: At projected peak, >=40% headroom on the bottleneck resource; scale-out triggers defined below saturation

Title: Validate linear/known scaling for capacity forecasting
Preconditions: Owned env; ability to run at multiple load levels
Test_Steps: 1. Run at 25/50/75/100% of peak | 2. Record utilization at each | 3. Fit utilization-vs-load curve | 4. Extrapolate to next growth tier
Expected_Result: Utilization scales predictably (no early non-linear knee); forecast for +50% growth produced; bottleneck named

Title: Degrade non-critical features first under resource pressure
Preconditions: Owned env; feature-priority/degradation policy defined; load injector
Test_Steps: 1. Apply pressure beyond comfortable capacity | 2. Observe which features degrade | 3. Confirm clinical core (chart/orders) stays available | 4. Confirm non-critical (analytics widgets) shed first
Expected_Result: Non-critical features degrade/disable first; clinical core remains usable; user sees clear degraded-mode messaging

Title: Decision table: dependency outage x load -> degradation response
Preconditions: Owned env; ability to fault-inject dependencies (DB replica, search, cache)
Test_Steps: 1. Enumerate dependency-down x load-level combinations | 2. Inject each | 3. Record degradation response and user impact | 4. Build table
Expected_Result: Each combination yields the documented degraded behavior; no full outage when a single non-critical dependency fails; failover honored

Title: Circuit breaker must open on slow dependency, not cascade fail
Preconditions: Owned env; circuit breaker configured on a dependency; latency injection
Test_Steps: 1. Inject high latency into a downstream dependency | 2. Drive load through it | 3. Verify breaker opens | 4. Confirm fast-fail not thread pile-up
Test_Data: Injected dep latency=5s; Breaker threshold set
Expected_Result: Breaker opens, requests fast-fail with fallback; no thread pool exhaustion/cascade; breaker half-opens and recovers when dependency heals

Title: FHIR Patient/Encounter read latency percentiles at volume
Preconditions: Owned env; FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; >=250k patients; percentile capture
Test_Steps: 1. Drive GET /fhir2/R4/Patient/{id} and /Encounter?patient= load | 2. Sustain 50 req/s 10 min | 3. Capture percentiles | 4. Validate resource conformance
Expected_Result: FHIR read p95<=1500ms, p99<=3000ms; responses conform to R4; no 5xx; bundle pagination links valid

Title: FHIR search with _count and pagination at boundary values
Preconditions: Owned env; FHIR endpoint; large Observation set seeded
Test_Steps: 1. Search Observation with _count at min, default, and max | 2. Follow next-page links | 3. Measure per-bundle latency | 4. Verify total and links
Expected_Result: Each _count honored within max cap; pagination links correct; per-bundle p95 within SLO; no unbounded bundle

Title: FHIR bulk export ($export) completion time and resource pressure
Preconditions: Owned env; FHIR bulk data ($export) supported or via Platform adapter; large cohort
Test_Steps: 1. Kick off group $export | 2. Poll status to completion | 3. Measure total time and peak resource use | 4. Verify NDJSON output integrity
Expected_Result: Export completes within budget; async kickoff returns 202 + status URL; no interactive SLO breach during export; NDJSON valid

Title: HL7v2 ADT message ingest throughput and queue drain
Preconditions: Owned env; HL7 inbound interface (MLLP) configured; message generator
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A04 stream at increasing rate | 2. Monitor inbound queue depth | 3. Find max sustained ingest rate with stable queue | 4. Verify ACK latency
Expected_Result: Sustained ingest rate documented; queue does not grow unbounded; ACK p95 within SLO; no message loss

Title: HL7 ingest backpressure and retry under burst
Preconditions: Owned env; HL7 interface with queue; burst generator
Test_Steps: 1. Send a burst above sustained ingest rate | 2. Observe queue buffering and processing catch-up | 3. Verify NAK/retry on overflow | 4. Confirm ordering preserved per patient
Expected_Result: Burst is buffered then drained; on overflow sender gets NAK/backpressure not silent drop; per-patient message ordering preserved

Title: CPOE order submission latency under concurrent prescriber load
Preconditions: Owned env; order entry enabled; active visits; prescriber tokens
Test_Steps: 1. Drive concurrent order POSTs (drug + lab) | 2. Sustain 30 prescribers 10 min | 3. Capture submit-to-confirm latency | 4. Verify no order loss
Expected_Result: Order submit p95<=1800ms; zero order loss; duplicate-order guard intact under concurrency; audit recorded

Title: Concurrent edits to same order list must not lose orders
Preconditions: Owned env; two sessions on same patient order set
Test_Steps: 1. Two clinicians add orders to same patient concurrently | 2. Save near-simultaneously | 3. Reload order list | 4. Verify both orders persisted, no last-write-wins loss
Expected_Result: Both orders persisted; optimistic locking or merge prevents silent overwrite; conflict surfaced if applicable; counts reconcile

Title: High-frequency vitals capture from multiple devices
Preconditions: Owned env; vitals encounter API; simulated device feed
Test_Steps: 1. Stream vitals observations from 50 simulated beds | 2. Sustain 15 min | 3. Capture write latency and persistence | 4. Verify obs datetime accuracy
Expected_Result: Vitals write p95<=1200ms; all observations persisted with correct timestamps; no dropped obs; chart reflects latest

Title: Concurrent booking of the same appointment slot
Preconditions: Owned env; appointment scheduling enabled; single open slot
Test_Steps: 1. Two users attempt to book the same slot simultaneously | 2. Submit concurrently | 3. Inspect slot state | 4. Verify single winner
Expected_Result: Exactly one booking succeeds; the other gets a clear slot-taken error; no double-book; slot state consistent

Title: Morning login storm at shift change
Preconditions: Owned env; auth + session subsystem; bulk credential set
Test_Steps: 1. Simulate 300 logins within 60s (shift change) | 2. Measure authenticate latency and error rate | 3. Verify session store scales | 4. Check downstream dashboard load
Expected_Result: Authenticate p95<=1500ms during storm; error rate<0.5%; session store not exhausted; first dashboard render within SLO

Title: Audit logging overhead under PHI-access load
Preconditions: Owned env; audit/PHI access logging enabled; load tool
Test_Steps: 1. Run PHI-access heavy load with audit on | 2. Repeat with audit off (lab only) | 3. Compare latency and throughput | 4. Verify no audit entries dropped
Expected_Result: Audit overhead quantified and acceptable (e.g. <10% latency add); zero audit entries dropped under load; audit writes durable

Title: Assistive-tech responsiveness during high server load
Preconditions: Owned env; screen reader (NVDA/VoiceOver) test station; ability to apply backend load
Test_Steps: 1. Apply heavy backend load | 2. Navigate key clinical screens with screen reader | 3. Measure focus/announcement timing | 4. Verify no missing live-region updates
Expected_Result: Under load, focus order and ARIA live announcements remain timely; no spinner traps; status updates announced; WCAG operability holds

Title: Auth/rate-limit controls must hold under load (no bypass)
Preconditions: Owned env; rate limiting + authz configured; load tool
Test_Steps: 1. Drive heavy authenticated + unauthenticated mixed load | 2. Attempt authz-protected calls without rights at high rate | 3. Verify rate limits and authz still enforced | 4. Check for race-condition bypass
Test_Data: Mixed auth/unauth; authz-violating calls at rate
Expected_Result: No authz bypass under load; rate limiting enforced consistently; no TOCTOU bypass; 401/403 returned reliably

Title: Exploratory profiling to discover unknown performance hotspots
Preconditions: Owned env; profiler/flame-graph tooling; representative load
Test_Steps: 1. Run representative load with continuous profiler | 2. Capture flame graphs and DB hot queries | 3. Explore top CPU/IO consumers | 4. Log unexpected hotspots and hypotheses
Expected_Result: At least 3 prioritized hotspots identified with evidence; each given a remediation hypothesis; no critical finding left unlogged

Title: Cold-start / post-restart latency and warm-up time
Preconditions: Owned env; ability to restart app node; warm-up metrics
Test_Steps: 1. Restart application node | 2. Immediately drive nominal load | 3. Measure latency from cold to warm | 4. Time until p95 reaches steady SLO
Expected_Result: Cold-start latency bounded; system transitions Cold->Warming->Steady; p95 reaches SLO within agreed warm-up window; no errors during warm-up

Title: Zero-downtime during rolling deploy under load
Preconditions: Owned env multi-node; rolling deploy pipeline; steady load
Test_Steps: 1. Apply steady clinical load | 2. Execute rolling deploy node-by-node | 3. Monitor error rate and latency during cutover | 4. Verify in-flight requests drained
Expected_Result: No error spike beyond threshold during deploy; in-flight requests drained, not killed; latency stays within SLO; zero failed clinical transactions

Title: Per-tenant/per-user throttling decision table
Preconditions: Owned env; throttling policy by tenant/user/endpoint configurable
Test_Steps: 1. Enumerate tenant-tier x endpoint-class x rate combinations | 2. Drive each past its limit | 3. Record throttle response | 4. Build table
Expected_Result: Each cell throttles at its configured limit; 429 with Retry-After returned; clinical-critical endpoints not over-throttled; table complete

Title: Bulk patient/data import throughput without interactive impact
Preconditions: Owned env; bulk import tool; off-peak and on-peak windows
Test_Steps: 1. Run a large bulk import (e.g. 100k records) | 2. Measure import throughput and DB load | 3. Run once isolated, once with interactive load | 4. Compare interactive p95
Expected_Result: Import throughput documented; with interactive load running, clinical p95 stays within SLO (import throttled/batched); no lock contention stalls
